archive_data.question_text: Which of the following is the key aspect of Constitutional bodies?

options.text: Made by the President and PM

explanation.english: Constitutional bodies are established and defined by the Constitution of India itself, which gives them permanent status and independent existence. They cannot be abolished or modified without amending the Constitution.
explanation.tanglish: Samvidhaana urupugal India Samvidhaanathalaye amaikkappattu, mudrichuvadikkappattu irukkum. Ivarkalukku staayana nilaiyum svatantra irupum undu. Samvidhaanatthai maatravendumaiyil ataiyal athigarikkavum izhithalikkavum mudiyaathu.

archive_data.question_text: Which State has the maximum strength of members in Lok Sabha?

explanation.english: Uttar Pradesh has 80 Lok Sabha seats, which is the highest among all states in India, followed by Maharashtra (48 seats) and West Bengal (42 seats).
explanation.tanglish: Uttar Pradeshil 80 Lok Sabha seattugal ullana, idhu Hindiyilulla anaithu maanilangalilum athigam. Thodarndhu Maharashtra (48 seattugal) matrum West Bengal (42 seattugal) ullana.

archive_data.question_text: What is the name of the phenomenon by which light splits into its component colours upon passing through a prism?

explanation.english: When white light passes through a prism, it splits into its constituent colors (VIBGYOR) due to different wavelengths refracting at different angles. This phenomenon is called dispersion of light.
explanation.tanglish: Velliya velicham prism vazhaga sendraal, vividha alai nilangal vividha konangalil vangugirathaal athu athan seyarkaiyana nirangalaga (VIBGYOR) pirigirathu. Indha iyarkaiye velichathin viraivu ena ariyappadugirathu.

archive_data.question_text: The dance-pavilion (mandapa), the vast complex within a quadrilateral precinct of the Odisha school of architecture, is known as _________.

explanation.english: In Odisha temple architecture, the Jagamohana is the dance pavilion or assembly hall (mandapa) where devotees gather. It is distinct from the Deul (sanctum sanctorum) and Vimana (tower).
explanation.tanglish: Odisha kovil kattidathil, Jagamohana endrum natana mandapam ena solappadum, idhu bakthargal koottai serum idam. Idhu Deul (sannithi) matrum Vimana (kottagai) idangalil irunthu vithyasamanaathu.

archive_data.question_text: Who among the following Buddhist monks came from China to India during the reign of Chandragupta II?

explanation.english: Fa-Hien (Faxian) visited India during the reign of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) in the early 5th century CE to collect Buddhist texts. Hiuen-Tsang visited during Harshavardhana's reign in the 7th century.
explanation.tanglish: Fa-Hien (Faxian) 5th century CEil Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) avargalin araachiyil bauddha grandangalai selavakki Indiavaruku payanam seithaar. Hiuen-Tsang 7th century CEil Harshavardhana araachiyil payanam seithaar.

archive_data.question_text: The Lingayats community of present-day Karnataka originated in the 12th century CE due to the emergence of a movement led by _________ .

explanation.english: Basavanna (Basaveshwara), a 12th-century philosopher and social reformer, founded the Lingayat movement in Karnataka. He advocated direct worship of Shiva without Brahminical rituals and caste discrimination.
explanation.tanglish: 12th century thathuvaviyalm matrum samudhaaya sudharakaraana Basavanna (Basaveshwara), Karnatakaavil Lingayat iyakkathai thottanginaar. Avar Brahmin rituals matrum jaathi vethikkai ingyaatha Shiva neradiya poojaiyai uruthee solliyaar.

archive_data.question_text: Which of the following movements was started by Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan?

explanation.english: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, also known as Frontier Gandhi, founded the Khudai Khidmatgars (Servants of God) in 1929. It was a non-violent resistance movement against British rule in the North-West Frontier Province.
explanation.tanglish: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Frontier Gandhi endrum ariyappaduvavar, 1929il Khudai Khidmatgars (Kadavulin uzhavanivar) ena iyakkathai thottanginaar. Idhu North-West Frontier Provinceil British aachiyukku ethiraana ahimsai poratta iyakkam.
